steps.title: Fastest path: identify the center first
steps.content: For coordinate-circle questions, read the center from the standard form and use the given point to compute the radius. Keep the center signs and the value of $r^2$ separate before choosing an equation.

steps.title: Recall the standard form of a circle
steps.content: A circle with center $(h,k)$ and radius $r$ has equation $$(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$$ With center $(-4,2)$: $h=-4$, $k=2$, so the equation starts as $$(x+4)^2+(y-2)^2=r^2$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $(x - 4)^2 + (y + 2)^2 = 10$: Center would be $(4,-2)$, not $(-4,2)$, and $r^2=10\neq 100$. ✗
  • Choice B — $(x + 4)^2 + (y - 2)^2 = 10$: Center $(-4,2)$ is correct, but $r^2=10\neq 100$. ✗
  • Choice C — $(x - 4)^2 + (y + 2)^2 = 100$: $r^2=100$ is correct, but center would be $(4,-2)$, not $(-4,2)$. ✗
  • Choice D — $(x + 4)^2 + (y - 2)^2 = 100$: Center $(-4,2)$ and $r^2=100$ both correct. That matches Choice D — $(x + 4)^2 + (y - 2)^2 = 100$. ✓
